passages.passage_text: Directions (Q. Nos. 2-3) Read the following information carefully to answer the questions that follow. I. 'A + B' means 'A is the son of B'. II. 'A - B' means 'A is the wife of B'. III. 'A × B' means 'A is the brother of B'. IV. 'A ÷ B' means 'A is the mother of B'. V. 'A = B' means 'A is the sister of B'.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: If 'A × D' means 'A is sister of D', 'A + D' means 'D is the daughter of A' and 'A ÷ D' means 'A is the mother of D', then how will 'A is the aunt of M' be denoted?

choices.a: P is the nephew of Q
choices.b: P is the uncle of Q
choices.c: P is the brother of Q
choices.d: P is the father of Q

solutions.solution: (a) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship. (a) (M + T – R) = Rejected [diagrams showing family trees] Here, gender of M is not known. So, M is either niece or nephew of R. (c) (R × T + M × J) = Rejected [diagram] M is the niece of R (d) (R + T – M + J) = Selected [diagram] M is the nephew of R Hence, only option (d) satisfies the given relation between M and R.

(e) By applying above decoding method, we can check all the options for the required relationship. (a) (W + T – H) = Rejected [diagrams showing family trees] W is grandmother of H (b) (W + T – H) = Rejected [diagram] W is the uncle of H (c) (W × T + H) = Rejected [diagram] W is the aunt of H (d) (W + T + H) = Rejected [diagram] W is uncle of H There is no option which satisfies the required relationship between W and H. So, option (e) is correct to answer.
